do_not_use_when: You want an AI/human verdict (the conformal p-value is a membership statistic, not P(AI))
Your calibration scores are not exchangeable with the target (the guarantee fails)
You have no calibration scores to supply

optional: --calibration-positive nonconformity scores for a positive class (two-class mode)
--fpr-bound q reference-class false-positive ceiling in (0, 1); emits the conformal threshold bounded by q (one-tailed direction only)
required: --calibration nonconformity scores for the reference class (JSON list or newline-delimited)
--score the target nonconformity score (required for the gate; optional with --fpr-bound, which can emit a threshold without a target)

artifacts: stdout markdown report (default), --json envelope, or --out PATH

purpose: A split-conformal abstention layer over any signal: given operator-supplied calibration nonconformity scores and a target score, emit a distribution-free, finite-sample conformal p-value and a prediction set at coverage 1-alpha. Turns "the band reads uncalibrated" into "abstain, with a guaranteed error rate at this operating point." A methodology wrapper, NOT a detector; an empty or full prediction set is a licensed abstention. The p-value is NOT P(AI). With --fpr-bound q it instead emits the conformal threshold whose reference-class false-positive rate is bounded by q under exchangeability (Multiscaled Conformal Prediction, arXiv:2505.05084) — a reference-class FPR ceiling the operator applies, not P(AI) and not a guarantee on the positive (AI) class.
use_when: You have calibration nonconformity scores for a reference class and want a guaranteed-coverage abstention on a target score
You want to convert an uncalibrated signal into a finite-sample, distribution-free decision
You want a two-class prediction set (supply --calibration-positive)
You want an operating point whose reference-class false-positive rate is bounded by a target ceiling q (supply --fpr-bound; one-tailed --direction only)
